name: All About Reading
description: Multi-sensory, Orton-Gillingham-based phonics and reading program with scripted lessons, letter tiles, and engaging readers
notes: Reading Interactive Kit ($22-44) is a one-time purchase used across all levels. Excellent for struggling readers and dyslexia. Mastery-based progression, not grade-locked. One-year money-back guarantee.

name: Logic of English Foundations
description: Comprehensive phonics, reading, spelling, and handwriting program that teaches the rules behind English spelling with multi-sensory methods
notes: Level A costs more ($228) due to Core Materials one-time purchase; subsequent levels $90-125. Online version available at ~$75/year. Orton-Gillingham based. Essentials series extends through grade 7 for older students. Teaches both cursive and print.

name: The Good and the Beautiful Language Arts
description: Beautifully illustrated, integrated language arts covering reading, grammar, spelling, writing, art, and geography in one program
notes: Many levels available as FREE PDF downloads. Non-denominational Christian with sporadic mentions of God and KJV references. Author is LDS but content is non-denominationally Christian. Placement test recommended as levels run advanced. Some reviewers note weak explicit phonics instruction and reading comprehension gaps.

name: Sonlight Language Arts
description: Literature-rich language arts integrated with grammar, mechanics, dictation, and creative writing using high-quality read-alouds and books
notes: Expensive but includes a mini-library of high-quality books reusable across siblings. Open-and-teach instructor guides with daily lesson plans. Flexible grade levels. One-year money-back guarantee. Heavy on reading — best for book-loving families. Returning customer discounts available.

name: Memoria Press Language Arts
description: Structured classical Christian language arts with emphasis on grammar, Latin, literature guides, and traditional spelling
notes: Complete classical curriculum with strong Latin component. Individual components can be purchased separately to customize. Grade-level packages include literature guides, spelling, grammar, and composition. More affordable than comparable classical programs like Veritas Press. Excellent customer service.

name: IEW Structure and Style
description: Systematic video-based writing curriculum teaching structure and style through incremental, mastery-based instruction
notes: Writing-focused — not a complete language arts program. Needs separate grammar, spelling, and reading programs (Fix It! Grammar available as add-on). Core Structure and Style program is secular-friendly; Christian-themed options also available. Video instruction means minimal parent prep for teaching, but initial learning curve to understand the method. Excellent return policy.

name: BookShark Language Arts
description: Secular, literature-based language arts covering reading, writing, grammar, spelling, vocabulary, and copywork using quality books
notes: Secular spin-off of Sonlight with the same literature-based approach. Four-day-per-week schedule. Uses Explode the Code for early phonics (levels A-C) and Wordly Wise for vocabulary (level C+). All-Subject bundles available at 20% discount. Diverse and modern literature selections.

name: Brave Writer
description: Charlotte Mason-inspired writing lifestyle curriculum using copywork, dictation, freewriting, and living literature to develop confident writers
notes: Not a traditional open-and-go curriculum — more of a writing lifestyle approach requiring significant parent involvement. Excellent for reluctant writers. Does NOT cover phonics or handwriting. Online classes ($100-200 per 3-week session) available but add to cost. Age-based bundles: Beginning (5-7), Emerging (8-10), Middle School, High School, College Prep.

name: Learning Language Arts Through Literature
description: Affordable, literature-based all-in-one language arts integrating phonics, grammar, spelling, writing, and reading through classic books
notes: Color-coded books by grade level. Based on Dr. Ruth Beechick's and Charlotte Mason's teaching ideas. Christian content is light and sporadic — some Bible passages, hymns for dictation. Many secular families use it comfortably. Gray Book (8th grade) is most overtly Christian. Composition coverage is considered light — advanced writers may need supplements. Literature books purchased separately.

name: Explode the Code
description: Simple, systematic Orton-Gillingham phonics workbook series that builds reading skills through incremental practice
notes: Budget-friendly phonics supplement, not a complete language arts program. 17 workbooks from primers through Book 8. Students can work mostly independently. Black and white illustrations (some hard to identify). Half-levels available for extra practice. Often used alongside BookShark, Charlotte Mason, or other literature-based programs.
